user_query:
I need you to schedule a small batch of events into my Apple Calendar based on the requests in input/scheduling_requests.json. Please read that file first. For each item, create an event on the specified calendar ("Work" or "Personal") using the relative day and a start time that falls within the provided time window, with the end time determined by the duration. Avoid any double-booking across all events occurring on the same relative day; if a conflict would occur, shift start times within the window to resolve it. Use the relative date exactly as provided in the file ("today", "tomorrow", "day after tomorrow", or a lowercase weekday name like "monday", "tuesday", etc.). For weekday-based items, pass the weekday name without the word "next". After creating the events, produce the following deliverables:

1) Write a newline-delimited JSON log to output/commands.jsonl, one JSON object per line with the exact arguments you used for each event, using these keys: calendar_name, event_summary, event_description, relative_start_date, start_time (HH:MM 24h), relative_end_date, end_time (HH:MM 24h). Use the same relative day for both start and end when the event is within a single day.

2) Write a summary file to output/summary.json with the structure: { "count": <int>, "events_by_day": { "today": <int>, "tomorrow": <int>, "day after tomorrow": <int>, "monday": <int>, "tuesday": <int>, "wednesday": <int>, "thursday": <int>, "friday": <int>, "saturday": <int>, "sunday": <int> } } (include only the keys actually used with integer counts).

3) Write output/notes.md explaining your scheduling choices, any adjustments you made to prevent overlaps, and any edge cases you encountered.

Important formatting rules:
- Keep times in 24-hour HH:MM format.
- Use the relative day string from the input exactly (lowercase and without the word "next").
- Ensure the scheduled start/end times stay fully within each request's window and match the specified duration.

The input file provides for each request: title, calendar ("Work" or "Personal"), relative_day, window_start, window_end, duration_minutes, and description. Please proceed and create the events, then produce the three output files listed above.
